title: Gospel of Thomas
goal: Accuracy-first translation prep with Coptic primary, Greek overlap, and Synoptic parallel awareness.

cross_reference_strategy.notes: Surface already-rendered Synoptic parallels later so Thomas can intentionally echo or diverge.

primary_witnesses.notes: Primary online Coptic/translation witness for Phase E bootstrapping.
Independent Coptic transcription to catch disagreements and confirm tricky readings.

Originally registered as a cross_check_coptic witness. OCR returned the Grondin PDF as Latin-transliteration (0 Coptic glyphs / 557-822 Latin chars per page), confirming that Grondin's edition is a romanized interlinear, NOT a Coptic-script critical edition. Kept here as a transliteration/alignment reference only; does NOT count as a second Coptic-script witness. Finding a true second Coptic-script Thomas witness (Gebhardt-Klein preferred) remains the open Thomas blocker.
Greek overlap witness for logia 1–7 from Agraphos.
Greek overlap witness for logia 26–33 from Agraphos.
Greek overlap witness for logia 24 and 36–39 from Agraphos.

guardrails: If a Greek fragment overlaps a saying and differs meaningfully from the Coptic, record both readings and defend the decision.
Treat Synoptic parallels as context, not as pressure to harmonize Thomas into the canonical gospels.
Keep odd or sharp Thomasine diction when the witness supports it instead of smoothing it into familiar church English.
